external_refs.description: CISA decision-tree framework that maps exploitation status, technical impact and mission/well-being impact to one of four actions (Track, Track*, Attend, Act). Designed as the action-oriented complement to CVSS severity.

FIRST.org open scoring framework producing a 0–10 severity score for a vulnerability across Base, Threat, Environmental and Supplemental metric groups. Severity ≠ priority; pair with EPSS / KEV / SSVC for action.
OASIS standard for representing CTI as typed objects (Indicator, Malware, Threat-Actor, Attack-Pattern, Relationship…) in JSON. Lingua franca for sharing structured threat data between platforms.

aka: Trusted Automated Exchange of Indicator Information
external_refs.description: OASIS HTTPS+JSON transport protocol for exchanging STIX bundles between producers and consumers via collections and channels. The wire format that pairs with STIX as the data format.
OASIS machine-readable format for vendor security advisories — supersedes older PDF/HTML advisories with structured JSON describing affected products, vulnerabilities, scores and remediations.
Google/OpenSSF JSON schema describing vulnerabilities in open-source packages with precise version-range affected/fixed semantics. Powers osv.dev, GHSA, PyPI Advisory DB and others.
Linux Foundation / ISO/IEC 5962 SBOM standard with a focus on licence compliance and provenance. Often required for US Federal supply-chain attestations alongside or instead of CycloneDX.
OWASP-led SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) specification. Covers components, dependencies, vulnerabilities, services, ML models and cryptographic assets in a single graph format.

OWASP requirements catalogue for verifying the security of web apps and services across three depth levels (L1 opportunistic, L2 standard, L3 advanced). Useful as a procurement / pen-test scope checklist.
OWASP testing playbook organised by category (auth, session, input-validation, business logic…). The practical companion to ASVS for hands-on web app pen-testing.
OWASP top-10 list of API-specific risks (BOLA, broken auth, over-permissive object property exposure, unrestricted resource consumption…). The API analogue to the classic OWASP Top 10.

Sysinternals system service that produces detailed Windows event log entries (process create with command line + hashes, network connections, image loads, registry, WMI). Foundation for most home-grown Windows EDR.
Windows Event Log channel where Sysmon writes its events (Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational). Forwarded via WEC/WEF or agent collectors into the SIEM.
MITRE ATT&CK data component for "process started" events (Sysmon EID 1, Windows Security 4688, Linux execve auditd). The single most useful telemetry for EDR detections.
MITRE ATT&CK data component for outbound/inbound network connection events (Sysmon EID 3, firewall/Zeek conn logs). Used for C2 detection, lateral movement and exfiltration analytics.
MITRE ATT&CK data component for DNS resolution events (Sysmon EID 22, DNS server query logs, passive-DNS). Powers DGA detection, DNS-tunnelling identification and threat-intel matching.

Windows audit event "A new process has been created". Includes parent process and (when Audit Process Creation Command Line policy is enabled) the full command line. Native equivalent of Sysmon EID 1.
Sysinternals real-time file-system, registry, process and network activity tracer. Standard tool for behavioural analysis of unknown binaries in a sandbox.
Sysinternals tool enumerating every Windows auto-start location (Run keys, services, scheduled tasks, drivers, WMI subscriptions, Office add-ins, AppInit DLLs, ...). Goes-to for persistence hunts.
Sysinternals enhanced Task Manager — process tree, loaded DLLs/handles, digital-signature verification, VirusTotal lookups. Quick triage for suspicious live processes.

Sysinternals command-line tool for capturing process memory dumps on triggers (CPU, exception, hang, time). Frequently abused by attackers to dump LSASS — and by responders to capture malware in flight.
Microsoft kernel and user-mode debugger. Standard tool for malware reverse engineering, crash-dump analysis and Windows kernel research.
Sysinternals binary signature / version-info verifier with optional VirusTotal hash lookup. Used to spot unsigned or mis-signed binaries in lieu of attestation tooling.
Microsoft cloud-native SIEM/SOAR built on Log Analytics. Ingests Microsoft 365, Azure, AWS, on-prem and third-party logs; detection rules in KQL; playbooks via Logic Apps.
Microsoft EDR/EPP platform — real-time process telemetry, behaviour analytics, attack-surface reduction rules and live-response shell on Windows/macOS/Linux endpoints.

Microsoft cloud identity platform — users, groups, conditional access, MFA, sign-in logs. Common pivot point for cloud incident response and a frequent target (token theft, illicit consent, dir-sync abuse).
Built-in K8s API-server audit log of every request (who, what, when, response). Critical telemetry for cluster-level threat detection — enable at Metadata or Request level and ship to a SIEM.
FIRST.org daily-updated probability (0–1) that a CVE will be exploited in the wild within the next 30 days. Used to triage which patches matter today vs. theoretically-severe-but-unexploited.
CISA-maintained list of CVEs with confirmed in-the-wild exploitation. KEV is the single strongest urgency signal — anything on the list should be patched ahead of equivalent-CVSS items that are not.
NIST high-level security programme framework with six Functions (Govern, Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover). Used for organisational maturity mapping and board-level reporting.
NIST incident-response lifecycle reference — Preparation, Detection & Analysis, Containment/Eradication/Recovery, Post-incident. Most enterprise IR runbooks are derived from this document.
Internal collection of curated Markdown/JSONL fixtures that bootstrap the knowledge graph with frameworks, products, tools and relationships. Lives under seed/knowledge/research_pack/.

Sysinternals system service that produces detailed Windows event log entries (process create with command line + hashes, network connections, image loads, registry, WMI). Foundation for most home-grown Windows EDR.
Microsoft kernel and user-mode debugger. Standard tool for malware reverse engineering, crash-dump analysis and Windows kernel research.
